item.title: Re: Guess it's time to post something
item.content:encoded: Yeah, it's too tall on the page. <.<

item.title: Re: Guess it's time to post something
item.content:encoded:
I keep getting e-mails about 3454 and lo and behold, it's been featured?! Thanks!?

Thank qntm, he selected it as the feature for his Reviewer Spotlight slot!

Somebody make it smaller for me.

Smaller as in make the image display less than 300 px wide? The filesize is already pretty small.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like where this is going, but reading it raises so many questions in my mind:

SCP-4181-2 cannot be moistened.

Cannot, should not, or must not?

Cannot = It's impossible. No matter how much water we pour on them, they remain dry.

+ show block
– hide block

Should not = You're not allowed to moisten them, per containment procedures. Some sort of disciplinary action or other penalty will be incurred if you're caught moistening them.

Must not = if we moisten them, some undesired behavior occurs. (E.G. termination or distortion of the SCP-4181-2 instance, aggressive behavior by SCP-4181-1, etc.)

SCP-4181-2 seem to possess only rudimentary sense of touch compared to an average human being.

How do we know that? There's nothing else in the 2D plane for them to touch, is there? We've established that they can't touch objects outside the surface. Do we ask them to touch themselves? Are there typically more than one SCP-4181-2 instance active at a time? Can they talk? Have we interviewed them after retrieval? If so, where are the interview logs? I'm very curious about their mental state.

Sapient SCP-4181-2 appear to possess the same memories and knowledge of the living being they resemble.

Again, I don't know whether they can talk or not. Did we determine this by observing their actions? Or can they simply tell us what they remember? If the latter, has a SCP-4181-2 ever been created from a Foundation personnel? (I.E. not D-class.)

SCP-4181-2 instances are animate, two-dimensional images that resemble the being attacked.

What do the lines look like they're made of? Chalk? Spraypaint? Clean vector art like a living cartoon character? What's the art style? What details are exaggerated? What details are abstracted away? Is there a limited color palette?

When imitating a movement that would normally occur in three dimensions — for example, moving the right arm across the chest to grasp the left arm — the motion will be replicated in two dimensions through the use of visual foreshortening. This has been used in the past as an excellent device for training Foundation forensic artists.

This is an oddly specific detail. Are they meant to look like silhouettes, or do they have details? Is there any limitation to this, or can they turn around 360 degrees?

It seems weird that the Foundation would use an SCP-4181-2 instance to train forensic artists. Yes, perspective and foreshortening are essential skills to master, but I assume any forensic artist employed by the Foundation would already have a working understanding of perspective and foreshortening because the Foundation tends to recruit the very best of the best. Unless they cross-trained into forensic art after joining the Foundation, there would never be a reason to do this, and it seems like such a minor skill to flirt with reclassifying SCP-4181-2 as Thaumiel.

More crucially, you don't learn foreshortening by copying someone else's stylized art. You learn that stuff by copying reality. Rotoscoping normal video footage would be more useful than SCP-4181-2 for teaching forensic artists how to foreshorten.


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

The longest surviving SCP-4181-2 instance was created in 1990 and expired in 2012.

Who was it made from? This sounds like a very significant character with a story to tell. Make some interview logs and tell it! Or at least explain why interviews are impossible and how we know the types of information you'd think would come from interviews.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was on the fence about this until the final collapsible about the -2 properties. While some of the facts are weird and fun, it's really just not building on anything above it for me. All in all, there are a lot of extra words which I feel could have been hammered down a lot in editing. Lot of reader attention (for me) was spent on details of function rather than moving along to more story.

Maybe it's my failings as a reader, but, this wandered a lot and struggled to really focus in on 'the point'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I don't trust my own translation skills. I leave it as an exercise for the reader!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only thing I would add is a footnote with the translation for the ritual incantation. Other than that, a very enthusiastic +1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so wacky! So it's a large bird with spatulas for a tail that whacks people so hard they turn into 2D cartoon characters, who are later used once a year when the bird wants to orchestrate its own "shows"?

I'll whack a +1 right in there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As the kids say these day, I gotchu fam. Hopefully it will be a little more apparent that that discrepancy is intentional…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only real nitpick I have is to make it explicit that a person spatula-whacked by the bird turns into a -2, because it took me a reread to realize that it didn't just appear out of thin air next to the guy.

Otherwise, it's a pleasantly weird little skip with a nice sense of internal mystery and enough sheer what-ness for me. For reasons I can't explain, it reminds me of SCP-100 somehow.

item.title: Guess it's time to post something
item.content:encoded:
I keep getting e-mails about 3454 and lo and behold, it's been featured?! Thanks!?

So it seemed like no better time to come out of 'retirement' (???) and coldpost another really stupid idea that I've been sitting on for literally less than a week, cuz that's what y'all seem to like. Enjoy.

Image is from Wikipedia, by Roderick Eime, licensed CC-BY-SA 3.0.
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lesser_Bird_of_Paradise.jpg

Somebody make it smaller for me.

Trivia
– hide block
  • I was originally going to call this "Spatula City" but decided not to because reasons.
  • Julio was my "Spanish name" in middle and high school Spanish class.
  • The Incantation was originally "Ravioli, ravioli, please take the spatuloli" but then I found out it's a Spongebob meme and didn't want to deal with the anachronism.
  • Seriously, that almost tanked the article in the final minute.
  • I hope everyone appreciates my Google Translated Spanish rhyming skills. It's not supposed to make sense. You can find the original rhyme here.
